sections.heading: Considerations
sections.body:
- To connect a Render-hosted application to an Atlas cluster,
  you must select AWS (Amazon Web Services) as the cloud provider for your Atlas
  cluster.

sections.heading: Create and Configure an Atlas Cluster
sections.body: To create and configure a cluster in the Atlas UI:

sections.heading: Step 1: Create an  account or sign in to .
sections.body: [Create an Atlas account or sign in to an existing account](https://account.mongodb.com/account/register).

sections.heading: Step 2: Go to the  dialog.
sections.body:
Do one of the following steps:

- Click **Build a Cluster**.

- Click **Create**.

sections.heading: Step 3: Select a cluster type.
sections.body: To learn more, see [Select Cluster Tier](create-cluster-instance).

sections.heading: Step 4: Specify a name for the cluster.
Step 6: Select a region.
sections.body:
Select the AWS (Amazon Web Services) region closest to the region where your
Render-hosted application is deployed.

| Render Region      | AWS Region                                    | Atlas region                                      |
| ------------------ | --------------------------------------------- | ------------------------------------------------- |
| Oregon, USA        | us-west-2                                     | US\_WEST\_2                                       |
| Ohio, USA          | Dedicated tier: us-east-2Flex tier: us-east-1 | Dedicated tier: US\_EAST\_2Flex tier: US\_EAST\_2 |
| Virginia, USA      | us-east-1                                     | US\_EAST\_1                                       |
| Frankfurt, Germany | eu-central-1                                  | EU\_CENTRAL\_1                                    |
| Singapore          | ap-southeast-1                                | AP\_SOUTHEAST\_1                                  |

sections.heading: Step 8: Specify how you would like to authenticate the connection to your  cluster.
sections.body:
In the **How would you like to authenticate your
connection?** section of the **Quickstart** page, you can
configure one of the following options for your cluster.

You can create environment variables for the following values in
your Render service that connects to Atlas.

Username and Password

Certificate

sections.heading: Step 8: Specify how you would like to authenticate the connection to your  cluster.
sections.body:
- Click **Username and Password**.

- Set the new user's **Username** and
  **Password**.

- Click **Create User**.

sections.heading: Step 8: Specify how you would like to authenticate the connection to your  cluster.
sections.body:
- Click **Certificate**. MongoDB uses X.509
  certificates for passwordless authentication.

- Specify the Common Name (CN) for the new user.

- *Optional*. Toggle the **Download certificate
  when user is added** to **On** to download the
  certificate after creating the user.

  If you choose to download the certificate, you must also
  specify certificate expiration. To specify the duration,
  choose the duration for the certificate from
  the dropdown. You can set the certificate expiration to
  `3`, `6`, `12`, or `24` months.

- Click **Add User**.

  The new user is granted [Project Data Access Read/Write](<Project Data Access Read/Write>) role by default.

sections.heading: Step 9: Specify from where you would like to connect to your  cluster.
sections.body:
You can enable access for any network that needs to read and write
data to your cluster. To enable access, you can configure access from the Render cloud environment to your cluster.

- Choose **Cloud Environment** to configure network
  access between Atlas and your Render environment.

- Add your Render service's static outbound IP addresses.

sections.heading: Step 11: Click  to view your cluster.
Connect to Your Render-Hosted Application
sections.body:
You can view a connection string in the Atlas UI and use it to
connect to your Render-hosted application.

sections.heading: Step 1: View your connection string in the Atlas UI.
sections.body:
- Click the **Connect** button for your cluster.

- Select the **Standard Connection** type.

- Click **Choose a connection method**.

- Click **Drivers**.

  To learn more, see [Drivers](drivers).

- Select the driver, install the driver, and copy the
  connection string.

To learn more, see
[Connection Strings](manual/reference/connection-string).

sections.heading: Step 2: Connect to your Render-hosted application.
sections.body:
- Return to your Render dashboard.

- Create environment variables to authenticate the connection to
  your Atlas cluster.

  To learn more, see [Environment Variables and Secrets](https://docs.render.com/configure-environment-variables)
  in the Render documentation.

- Add the connection string to your application code.
